text: What do you or I need to model us together when considered in the vastness of space and dying? There can never be another day, for so many reasons. And there shouldn't be. He's a public servant. He feels that he had the unique opportunity to be the the voice for nature, to tell everybody about the wonders of nature, how important nature is. We should not just be fascinated by it but treasure it and look after it. The ethos that he has in his work is saying, like, look at the rest of the world, look at the collective and what we can do to help better that and stop thinking so individually about what affects us. Mainly you just feel incredibly blessed to have been alive at the same time. And his work will live on for decades. I think he's like a national treasure in every way. The amount of people that watch all his shows and learn something from them is still so integral. I can't believe he's a portrait. Wow. He defines what is good about Britain, what's good about being a human being. I think that's why he says that. We have to think about things that are difficult and we have to think about things that are painful because if we do not, there's going to be a bigger difficulty and a bigger pain that's waiting around the corner.
